title: Port FlexiDB core classes
description:
Port the FlexiDB in-memory indexed database and supporting classes. This is a prerequisite for Phase 5 (SprintReportTeamAnalysis). Includes:
- FlexiDB.groovy (~292 lines) - main database with indexing
- FlexiDBRow - dict-like row with defaults
- FlexiDBQueryColumn, FlexiDBIndexKey - query/index support
- Exception classes
- Init classes for column definitions

title: Port IndividualContributionsAnalysis to Python
description:
Port IndividualContributionsAnalysis.groovy (~1,156 lines) to Python. Second major analysis class after SprintReportTeamAnalysis.

Key components:
- Similar structure to SprintReportTeamAnalysis
- aggregateData() with issue/PR/commit aggregation
- Parallel processing with ThreadPoolExecutor
- Dense JSON navigation and counter increments
- CSV output generation

Depends on completion of SprintReportTeamAnalysis patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Analysis class ported with tests passing

title: Port SprintReportTeamAnalysis to Python
description:
Port SprintReportTeamAnalysis.groovy (~1,050 lines) to Python. This is the first of two main analysis classes.

Key components:
- Constants for user filtering, merge commit detection, PR tracking
- aggregateData() with SCRUM/KANBAN support and caching
- getIssueCategoryInformation() with nested PR/commit/comment processing
- generateOutput() producing per-sprint and overall CSV totals
- Helper methods: processDiffs(), processComment() (recursive), incrementCounter()
- CommentBlocker inner class for tracking
- Parallel issue processing with ThreadPoolExecutor

This is high-volume code with extensive JSON dot-notation navigation (~1500 lines similar patterns in the second analysis file).
acceptance_criteria: Analysis class ported with tests passing

title: Port ManagerTools to Python
description: Port the entire Groovy codebase (~4600 lines, 25 files) to Python. All output goes under python/ at repo root to avoid conflicts with existing Groovy source. Order: FlexiDB -> RestService/Auth -> REST clients -> AbstractSprintReport -> analysis files.
notes:
Phase 4 complete: CLI infrastructure (ConfigFileManager, CommandLineHelper, NullREST, GetTeamSprints, AbstractSprintReport) with 16 new tests. All 55 tests passing.

Completed phases:
1. FlexiDB (indexed in-memory database)
2. RestService/AuthInfo (HTTP client and auth)
3. REST clients (GitHub, Bitbucket, Jira)
4. CLI infrastructure (abstract base + config/prompts)

Remaining:
5. Analysis files (SprintReportTeamAnalysis ~1050 lines, IndividualContributionsAnalysis ~1150 lines) - high volume of dot-notation JSON navigation

title: Review entire code base for suggested refactors
notes: Full audit complete. 50+ issues found across 10 categories. Top priorities: (1) Massive code duplication in IndividualContributionsAnalysis - processGitHubRepository/processBitBucketRepository are 140+ lines of near-identical code; (2) getIssueCategoryInformation() in SprintReportTeamAnalysis is 250 lines - needs split; (3) Pattern.quote() security fix in JiraCopyTicketEstimates; (4) Delete unused getRequestConfig() in RestService; (5) Apply @Canonical to FlexiDBQueryColumn; (6) Use Groovy sort idiom in JiraREST; (7) Remove commented-out dead code; (8) Unsafe null checks missing in several places. Full issue list recorded in ManagerTools-0yu via apply-suggestions task.

title: Create Python port documentation and setup guide
description:
Create comprehensive documentation for running the Python ManagerTools port.

Scope:
- Update requirements.txt with all Python dependencies (requests/httpx, pytest, etc.)
- Create python/README.md with setup and usage instructions:
  - Virtual environment setup
  - Dependency installation
  - How to run each analysis class (once ported)
  - Configuration file location and format
  - Authentication setup (Jira, GitHub, Bitbucket)
- Add 'Python Port' section to CLAUDE.md explaining:
  - Architecture differences from Groovy
  - Module structure
  - Running tests
- Create example scripts showing:
  - FlexiDB usage patterns
  - How to instantiate and run analysis classes
  - Output format examples

Target: Completion after SprintReportTeamAnalysis and IndividualContributionsAnalysis are ported.
